project.value: Escola Sabatina operational and mobile-first attendance app
project.source: User requests and existing Next.js app structure
project.rationale: The app manages classes, students, attendance, points, offerings, responsibilities and guardian flows.

visual_direction.value: Editorial brutalist system with cream background, white surfaces, black 4px borders, hard offset shadows, strong flat accents and polaroid-style student media
visual_direction.source: docs/ui-audit.md, app/globals.css and recent attendance UI work
visual_direction.rationale: This direction is already established across forms, dashboard cards and the new mobile attendance flow.

governance_preset.source: User asked for app-wide standardization and primary use is mobile
governance_preset.rationale: The app is a workflow tool used repeatedly on phones and desktop.

strictness.source: Existing code still has many inline Tailwind arbitrary values
strictness.rationale: New work should use tokens, while old screens can migrate incrementally without blocking delivery.

accessibility_baseline.value: WCAG 2.2 AA intent with visible focus, 44px touch targets, labels and keyboard-closeable dialogs
accessibility_baseline.source: TRUE GOLD workflow and mobile attendance requirements
accessibility_baseline.rationale: The app is operated in class settings, often on mobile, so touch and focus states must be reliable.

scoring_period_governance.value: Classes are permanent and scoring periods are explicit, selectable, status-bearing records with an audit workflow
scoring_period_governance.rationale: Teachers must start a new count without losing access to a previous quarter that is still pending audit.

period_status_language.value: Programado, Em andamento, Encerrado - aguardando auditoria, Auditoria em andamento, and Auditado - somente leitura
period_status_language.rationale: Operational and audit states must be understandable without relying on color alone.
